findings.title: End-of-Life Apache Kafka Migration Risk
findings.description: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8 poses a critical risk due to its end-of-life status and known vulnerabilities. Migration to Kafka 3.7+ is essential to ensure platform reliability and security.
assumptions: Migration to Kafka 3.7+ will resolve existing vulnerabilities.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from Apache Kafka 2.x to Kafka 3.7+ to eliminate vulnerabilities.

metadata.mitigation: Complete the migration to Kafka 3.7+ to eliminate vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Vendor Lock-in Risk with ETL SDK
findings.description: DataPipe is facing a critical risk due to reliance on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK, which is set to increase in cost significantly. This dependency could hinder flexibility and increase operational costs.
assumptions: Evaluating alternatives will mitigate vendor lock-in.

evidence.excerpt: Assess open-source alternatives to VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK.

recommendations: Evaluate open-source alternatives to mitigate vendor lock-in.

metadata.mitigation: Assess and transition to open-source alternatives to mitigate vendor lock-in.

findings.title: Python 3.9 EOL Migration Requirement
findings.description: The impending end-of-life for Python 3.9 necessitates an upgrade to Python 3.12 across multiple services. This presents technical challenges due to dependency incompatibilities that need to be addressed to maintain security and functionality.
assumptions: Upgrading to Python 3.12 will resolve compatibility issues.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate all pipeline worker services from Python 3.9 to Python 3.12.

metadata.mitigation: Plan and execute the upgrade to Python 3.12 to ensure continued support.

findings.title: Deprecated Apache Kafka Migration Risk
findings.description: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8, which is reaching end-of-life in Q1 2026, poses a critical risk due to known vulnerabilities and incompatibility with the new KRaft consensus protocol.
assumptions: Migration is feasible within the timeline.

evidence.excerpt: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8 poses significant risks due to its end-of-life status.

recommendations: Migrate to Kafka 3.7+ before the end-of-life date.

metadata.mitigation: Complete the migration to Kafka 3.7+ before Q1 2026.

findings.title: Redis Cluster Migration Dependency
findings.description: The current single-region Redis cluster needs to be replaced with a multi-region solution to address latency issues and prepare for Redis 6 EOL in March 2026.
assumptions: A multi-region solution will resolve latency issues.

evidence.excerpt: The current single-region Redis cluster needs to be replaced with a multi-region solution.

recommendations: Evaluate Redis 7+ or alternative caching solutions.

metadata.mitigation: Plan for migration to a multi-region Redis solution before March 2026.

summary: Identified 5 risks (1 compliance, 4 security). 3 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (3 blockers, 0 warnings).
